records.ety: [OE. schamel a bench, stool, AS. scamel, sceamol, a bench, form, stool, fr. L. scamellum, dim. of scamnum a bench, stool.]

defs: One of a succession of niches or platforms, one above another, to hold ore which is thrown successively from platform to platform, and thus raised to a higher level.
A place where butcher's meat is sold.
quotes: As summer flies are in the shambles.

defs: A place for slaughtering animals for meat.
quotes: To make a shambles of the parliament house.

records.ety: [Cf. OD. schampelen to slip, schampen to slip away, escape. Cf. Scamble, Scamper.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Shambled (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Shambling (?).]

defs: To walk awkwardly and unsteadily, as if the knees were weak; to shuffle along.
